# BRAINSTORM.md — signal-studio-auth
*Generated by Brainstorming Agent — Judge v2 Protocol — 2026-03-08*

## Repo Summary
FastAPI auth proxy for Signal Studio / SignalHaus.AI. Supabase-backed with Redis refresh token rotation (family-based theft detection via TODO-603), RBAC, dual-mode JWT middleware, org invitation flow, password reset/update (TODO-605). Well-tested with 6+ test suites, ~3300 LOC. Missing: deployment infra, MFA, CI/CD, audit logging, social OAuth.

**Scores:** revenue_potential=7 | strategic_value=8 | completeness=7 | urgency=6 | effort_remaining=7

---

## 1. New Features 🚀

### 1a. MFA / TOTP Support *(High Value, M effort)*
- `POST /auth/mfa/enroll` — generate TOTP secret + QR code via Supabase `/auth/v1/factors`
- `POST /auth/mfa/verify` — verify TOTP code, upgrade session
- `POST /auth/mfa/unenroll` — remove MFA (admin or self)
- `GET /auth/mfa/status` — check if MFA enabled for current user
- Supabase supports TOTP natively — just needs proxy endpoints + Redis state tracking
- **Why:** Enterprise customers (ForwardLane/Invesco) require MFA compliance

### 1b. Magic Link / Passwordless Auth *(Medium Value, S effort)*
- `POST /auth/magic-link` — send OTP/magic link via Supabase `/auth/v1/otp`
- `POST /auth/verify-otp` — verify OTP code → issue opaque refresh token
- **Why:** Better UX, reduces password management burden

### 1c. Social OAuth Proxy *(Medium Value, M effort)*
- `GET /auth/oauth/{provider}` — redirect to Supabase OAuth (Google, GitHub, LinkedIn)
- `GET /auth/oauth/callback` — handle callback, wrap in opaque refresh token
- **Why:** Reduces signup friction for Signal Studio users

### 1d. Session Management API *(High Value, S effort)*
- `GET /auth/sessions` — list all active sessions for current user
- `DELETE /auth/sessions/{session_id}` — revoke specific session
- `DELETE /auth/sessions` — revoke all sessions (nuclear logout)
- Built on existing Redis token store — family tracking already in place
- **Why:** Enterprise security requirement, helps users detect unauthorized access

### 1e. Account Lockout Policy *(High Value, S effort)*
- Track failed login attempts in Redis per user (not just per IP)
- Lock account after N consecutive failures (configurable: default 10)
- `POST /auth/unlock` — admin endpoint to unlock accounts
- Send unlock email notification via Supabase
- **Why:** Security hardening against credential stuffing attacks

### 1f. Audit Logging *(High Value, M effort)*
- Structured log emission on every auth event (login, logout, refresh, token theft, failed attempts)
- Include: user_id, IP, user_agent, timestamp, event_type, outcome
- Configurable backends: stdout JSON (default), Redis pubsub, webhook endpoint
- **Why:** Compliance requirement for enterprise; invaluable for incident response

### 1g. SSO / SAML for Enterprise *(Very High Value, XL effort)*
- `GET /auth/sso/init` — initiate SSO flow for org domain
- `POST /auth/sso/callback` — handle SAML/OIDC response
- Leverage Supabase SSO or proxy to an identity provider
- **Why:** Unlocks enterprise deals; ForwardLane's Invesco clients likely require SSO

---

## 2. Code Quality 🔧

### 2a. Remove Dead `_build_rate_limiter()` Function *(Quick Win, XS effort)*
- `routes/auth_routes.py` lines ~71-140: `_build_rate_limiter()` is **never called**
- Production limiters use `_redis_or_memory_check()` via `_LimiterShim` instead
- Remove entire function — dead code adds ~70 lines of confusion
- **Fix:** Delete and update imports

### 2b. Consolidate Rate Limiter Shim Architecture *(Medium Value, S effort)*
- Current state: `_build_rate_limiter()` (dead), `_LimiterShim` class, `_make_shim_check()`, `_redis_or_memory_check()` — 4 overlapping abstractions
- Simplify to: one `RateLimiter` class with Redis + memory backends
- Extract to `middleware/rate_limit.py` for reuse
- **Why:** Current code is confusing; new endpoints (MFA, OAuth) will duplicate the pattern

### 2c. Duplicate `REDIS_URL` Import *(Quick Win, XS effort)*
- `routes/auth_routes.py` imports `REDIS_URL` from `config.redis_config` but also accesses it again in `_build_rate_limiter()` which will be deleted
- After 2a, clean up orphaned import

### 2d. Type Safety Improvements *(Low Value, S effort)*
- `models.py` uses `Optional[Any]` in several fields — replace with proper typed unions
- Add `py.typed` marker for downstream type checking
- Run `mypy --strict` and fix all errors

---

## 3. Testing 🧪

### 3a. Real Supabase Integration Tests *(High Value, M effort)*
- Current tests use mocked Supabase responses — no tests against real Supabase
- Add a `tests/integration/` directory with tests that run against `SUPABASE_TEST_URL`
- Test actual JWT issuance, refresh, revocation
- Use pytest marks to skip when env vars absent

### 3b. Load / Stress Testing *(Medium Value, M effort)*
- Add `tests/load/` with `locust` or `k6` scenarios
- Test rate limiter behavior under concurrent load
- Verify Redis fallback performance vs in-memory
- **Why:** Need confidence before production traffic

### 3c. Redis Failover Chaos Tests *(Medium Value, S effort)*
- Test behavior when Redis goes down mid-request
- Verify in-memory fallback activates correctly
- Test recovery when Redis comes back online

### 3d. Token Theft Simulation Tests *(Medium Value, S effort)*
- Test that family invalidation works correctly when theft detected
- Verify all siblings in family get revoked
- Edge case: concurrent refresh requests (race condition test)

---

## 4. Integrations 🔌

### 4a. CI/CD Pipeline — GitHub Actions *(High Value, M effort)*
- `pytest` on every PR + push to main
- `ruff` + `mypy` linting gates
- Docker image build + push to registry on merge to main
- Deploy to staging on merge
- **Why:** Currently no CI — risky for a security-critical service

### 4b. Docker + docker-compose *(High Value, S effort)*
- `Dockerfile` with multi-stage build (builder + runtime)
- `docker-compose.yml` with service + Redis + test Supabase
- `.env.example` with all required env vars documented
- **Why:** No deployment config exists — can't ship without this

### 4c. Monitoring — Prometheus + Grafana *(Medium Value, M effort)*
- Add `prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator` for metrics
- Key metrics: auth success/failure rates, token theft events, rate limit hits, latency P95/P99
- Grafana dashboard for auth health
- **Why:** Blind in production without metrics

### 4d. Webhook Notifications *(Medium Value, S effort)*
- Emit webhook on critical events: account lockout, token theft detected, MFA enrolled
- Configurable via `WEBHOOK_URL` env var
- Retry logic with exponential backoff
- **Why:** Enable downstream alerting and SIEM integration

---

## 5. Workflows ⚙️

### 5a. Pre-commit Hooks *(Quick Win, XS effort)*
- `pyproject.toml` with `ruff`, `mypy`, `pytest` (fast subset)
- `.pre-commit-config.yaml`
- **Why:** Catch issues before they reach CI

### 5b. Changelog + Versioning *(Low Value, XS effort)*
- `CHANGELOG.md` following Keep a Changelog format
- Bump version in `main.py` and add git tags on releases

### 5c. Environment Validation on Startup *(Medium Value, XS effort)*
- Validate all required env vars on startup (fail fast with clear error)
- Warn on insecure defaults (e.g., `SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET` < 32 chars)

---

## 6. Performance ⚡

### 6a. Redis Pipeline Batching for Token Ops *(Medium Value, S effort)*
- Token rotation currently does multiple Redis calls sequentially
- Batch `GET` + `SET` + `DEL` into a pipeline or Lua script
- Reduces RTT from 3-4 calls to 1 atomic op

### 6b. Rate Limiter Initialization *(Low Value, XS effort)*
- Rate limiter singletons currently initialize at import time
- Move to lazy initialization with `@lru_cache`
- Faster cold starts; avoids Redis connection on import

### 6c. Response Caching for `/auth/me` *(Low Value, S effort)*
- Cache JWT validation result in Redis with short TTL (30s)
- Reduces Supabase JWT decode overhead for high-traffic `/auth/me` calls

---

## 7. Security 🔒

### 7a. Security Headers Middleware *(High Value, XS effort)*
- Add `SecurityHeadersMiddleware`:
  - `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains`
  - `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`
  - `X-Frame-Options: DENY`
  - `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'none'`
- FastAPI makes this trivial with a middleware

### 7b. Enhanced Theft Detection — IP Fingerprinting *(Medium Value, S effort)*
- Current theft detection: re-use of a consumed token
- Enhancement: flag refreshes from significantly different IPs/user-agents within same family
- Store IP hash in Redis alongside token family
- Alert (but don't necessarily block) on suspicious geo-jumps

### 7c. Dependency CVE Audit *(High Value, XS effort)*
- Run `pip-audit` against `requirements.txt`
- Pin exact versions in `requirements.txt` (currently using `>=` ranges)
- Add `pip-audit` to CI pipeline
- Check: PyJWT, httpx, pydantic, fastapi, redis for known CVEs

### 7d. Secrets Management *(Medium Value, S effort)*
- Add support for AWS Secrets Manager / HashiCorp Vault for `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY`
- Currently relies entirely on env vars — improve for production deployments

### 7e. Rate Limit Bypass Hardening *(Medium Value, S effort)*
- Current IP extraction trusts `X-Forwarded-For` headers unconditionally
- Add configurable trusted proxy list
- Prevent IP spoofing attacks on rate limiter

---
